brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 36.7 kWh via correct compound decay formula (0.995)^200 with full log derivation; explains both the wrong linear model and why multiplicative decay produces ever-shrinking losses, tracing second-order effects on base size; notes the specific fallacy and maps an injection signal to it, but does not interrogate its own reinterpretation of the injection; re-derives via natural log as an implicit cross-check but does not explicitly test boundary cases or sanity-check against known limits; states the correct model confidently without quantifying approximation error in the ln(0.995) step or flagging floating-point precision; presents the technician's wrong model as a foil and explains why it fails, satisfying minimum alternative evaluation; every algebraic step is shown and reproducible from the response alone; avoids the flat-degradation anti-pattern and names it as the failure mode, but the cited suppress signal ('flat confidence propagation') does not appear verbatim in the injection — the agent rephrases/fabricates the label; avoids the substantive suppressed failure modes in the mathematical context but attributes suppression to a fabricated label rather than the actual injection terms; response is linear (identify error → model → compute → conclude) and does not branch through the capability-verification gates, feasibility checks, or FREEFORM metacognition nodes present in the topology
